'''Whuffa worms''' were long [[Worm/Legends|worms]] that inhabited the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]]. They were intelligent and had hydrostatic skeletons.

==Biology and appearance==
Wuffua worms were not asexual like most worms, and had [[Gender/Legends|males and females]]. When the female was ready to [[Reproduction/Legends|mate]], she would leave a trail of semi-viscous that would attract males. They burrowed through the crust of the planet and ate the [[Ore/Legends|minerals]] in the ground.<ref name="CTD" />

==History==
[[File:GiantWuffaWorm-FFp22.png|thumb|left|250px|A Giant Whuffa attacks an excavation site.]]
Giant whuffa worms were bred by the [[Kwa/Legends|Kwa]] to act as [[Temple Guardian]]s for the [[Infinity Gate]].<ref name="Star Wars (1998) 24">[[Star Wars (1998) 24|''Star Wars'' (1998) 24]]</ref>

Thinner whuffa worms were hunted by the clans of Dathomir in extensive rituals. The clans on Dathomir used their skin to make their armor and dried hide as rope. The meat was used to make [[Stew/Legends|stew]].<ref name="CTD" />

When [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] was on Dathomir, he was included in one of the whuffa retrieving rituals for the [[Singing Mountain Clan]].<ref name="The Courtship of Princess Leia">''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]''</ref> When [[Nuru Kungurama]], [[Lalo Gunn]], the Blackhole Pirates, Professor Groob (and his family) and the Breakout Squad were escaping [[Plunder Moon]] in an Infinity Gate they were attacked by a whuffa worm but escaped.<ref name="TCWSM2">''[[The Clone Wars: Secret Missions 2: Curse of the Black Hole Pirates]]''</ref>
